components.prompt: Read flow.py to understand current CLI structure. Identify: (a) where cmd_list renders output, (b) where to add describe_workflow(), (c) where to wire cmd_sum into the argument parser.
Does cmd_list currently show natural language descriptions?
Implement describe_workflow() that walks components in execution order and generates an arrow-connected sentence. Then enhance cmd_list to print the description alongside dialog/logic counts.
Does cmd_list output look correct with descriptions for existing workflows?

Implement cmd_sum: scan workspace (git log, README, directory tree, existing flows), auto-generate a .Flow.json workflow with Plan→Logic→Build→Logic→Goal components, handle --force overwrite, --print-context preview. Wire into arg parser.
Does flow sum generate a valid, check-passing workflow from this workspace?
Update SKILL.md in ~/.config/opencode/skills/flow/ with the new list (descriptions) and sum commands.
Goal: flow list shows natural language descriptions. flow sum generates a valid workspace-aware workflow. Both pass flow check. Documented in SKILL.md.
